Section.name: UNCLASSIFIED
Section.Content: SD - Secure Workplace Access Prepared by Arjan Vroege Date 1/04/2025 Cegeka reference [Comments] CONFIDENTIAL Cegeka nv Corda 3, Kempische Steenweg 307 B-3500 Hasselt www.cegeka. com

Section.name: 0 VERSION CONTROL
Section.Content: General Document Secure Workplace Access – Service Description Owner of document Arjan Vroege Date 10-10-2025 Current version 1.1 Version Control Table Version Date Remarks / change log Author 0.1 20-02-2025 Initial Version Arjan Vroege 0.9 21-03-2025 Review Product Managers processed Arjan Vroege 1.0 28-03-2025 Final review processed Arjan Vroege 1.1 24-10-2025 Rename and Rebranding / Update Service Requests Arjan Vroege Reviewers Version Distribution to – Name of person Function / Role 0.1 Tom Speijer, Bart Weemaels, Bart Swerts, Gert Vanmechelen Product Managers DEX 0.9 Joeri Jordens, Jeroen Debehets, Bram Pirotte Engineer + Product Delivery Owner Approval Version Approval date Approved by (name person + role/function 1.0 28-03-2025 Product Managers DEX Reference Version Date Name Document Remarks Table of Contents 0 VERSION CONTROL 2 1 Service Description 4 1.1 Service Summary 4 1.2 Services 5 1.2.1 Standard Services 5 1.2.2 Optional Services 6 1.2.3 Service Request 6 1.2.4 Out of scope 8 1.3 Service Characteristics 8 1.3.1 Obligatory Services 8 1.3.2 Prerequisites & Conditions 8 1.3.3 Cegeka owned licenses and/or subscriptions 8 1.3.4 Customer owned licenses and/or subscriptions 8 1.3.5 Location 9 1.3.6 Ownership 9 1.3.7 Miscellaneous 9 1.3.8 RACI Matrix 9 2 Onboarding 10 3 Service Levels Management 12 3.1 Service Window 12 3.2 Service Level 12 4 Service Billing 13 4.1 Standard Service Billing 13 4.2 Optional Service Billing 14 4.3 Service Request Billing 14 5 Relation to Enabling Services 16

Section.name: Service Summary
Section.Content: The Cegeka Secure Workplace Access Service focuses on providing secure connectivity towards the customers Microsoft 365 tenant, internet and private resources. This service is mainly focusing on the Microsoft 365 services which provide these capabilities. Microsoft Global Secure Access is a comprehensive solution designed to secure your organization's Microsoft 365, internet and private access needs. By integrating Microsoft Entra Internet Access and Microsoft Entra Private Access, it provides a seamless, identity-aware, and cloud-delivered network perimeter. This ensures secure access to applications and data from anywhere, enhancing your security posture with Zero Trust principles. Zero Trust is a security strategy that assumes no implicit trust and verifies every request as though it originates from an open network. The core principles of Zero Trust are: These principles are integral to the Secure Access Service Edge (SASE) architecture, Microsoft Entra Internet Access and Microsoft Entra Private Access - coupled with Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps - are uniquely built as a solution that converges network, identity, and endpoint access controls so you can secure access to any app or resource, from anywhere. This service gives the customer the following business value to his Digital Employee eXperience: The Secure Workplace Access service provided by Cegeka maintains the customer's Global Secure Access environment and offers proactive recommendations for improvements. Additionally, Cegeka will provide usage insights through a dashboard that contains all essential information, giving the customer a comprehensive overview of the service's usage.

Section.name: Standard Services
Section.Content: The following base services will be delivered as part of this service: Maintain the Global Secure Access management platform; Provide pro-active advice about the use of Global Secure Access; Maintain the Microsoft 365 Traffic Profile within Global Secure Access; Maintain and keep Global Secure Access clients up to date; Only when not integrated in the core of the corresponding operating system; Provide Dashboard of the Global Secure Access Platform including the following information: Alerts and Notifications (last 24 hours); Client Activity Status Traffic Insights; Technical L2/L3 incident handling for Global Secure Access Platform and the Microsoft 365 traffic profile & Private Access

Section.name: Optional Services
Section.Content: The following optional service can be added to the standard services as part of this service: Internet Access The following additional services will be delivered as part of this additional service: Maintain Internet Access Traffic Profile within Global Secure Access; Maintain Internet Access Features like: Security Profile Web content Filtering Policies Provide Dashboard of the Internet Access including the following information: Alerts and Notifications (last 24 hours); Traffic Insights for Internet Access; Additional Technical L2/L3 incident handling for Entra Internet Access Platform Private Access The following additional services will be delivered as part of this additional service: Maintain Private Access Features like: Quick Access with or without Private DNS; Application Discovery Enterprise Applications Conditional Access support for applications within Private Access Maintain Private Access Traffic Profile within Global Secure Access Maintain on Private Access Connectors; Updating the connectors with at least n-1 version of the connector software; High availability of the connector groups (at least 2 connectors per group); Provide Dashboard of the Private Access including the following information: Alerts and Notifications (last 24 hours); Connector Status Traffic Insights Private Access;

Section.name: Service Request
Section.Content: A Service Request can be a • Request for a Standard Change • Request for a Standardized Change • Request for a Non Standard Change • Request for Information Table below contains all Service Requests for this Service, id TYPE SERVICE REQUEST SERVICE LEVEL TARGET STANDARD CHANGE None STANDARDIZED CHANGE None non STANDARD CHANGE Non Standard Change Install / Update Global Secure Access Client version 3 days Non Standard Change Remove / Rollback Global Secure Access Client version 3 days Non Standard Change Create Security Profile 2 days Non Standard Change Update Security Profile 2 days Non Standard Change Remove Security Profile 2 days Non Standard Change Create Web Content Filtering Rule 2 days Non Standard Change Update Web Content Filtering Rule 2 days Non Standard Change Remove Web Content Filtering Rule 2 days Non Standard Change Create Connector Group 2 days Non Standard Change Install and add new Connector 5 days Non Standard Change Update Connector(s) 2 days Non Standard Change Create Application 2 days Non Standard Change Grant Access to existing Application 2 days Non Standard Change Remove Access to existing Application 2 days Non Standard Change Remove Application 2 days Non Standard Change Remove and delete Connector 5 days Non Standard Change Remove Connector Group 2 days A Request for Information is not part of the Standard or Optional Services and will be charged as an Incident Ticket. Detailed definitions of all Service Types can be found in the ‘Enabling Services Document’, cfr. Chapter ‘Relation to Enabling Services’.

Section.name: Out of scope
Section.Content: Everything that is not explicitly mentioned in this service description is by default out of scope.

Section.name: Obligatory Services
Section.Content: The following service are Obligatory for this service to be delivered:

Section.name: Prerequisites & Conditions
Section.Content: This service has the following prerequisites and conditions before this service can be delivered: “Application Administrator” “Global Secure Access Administrator” “Global Reader”

Section.name: Cegeka owned licenses and/or subscriptions
Section.Content: N/A

Section.name: Customer owned licenses and/or subscriptions
Section.Content: These licenses and/or subscriptions and not included in this Service. It is the Customer responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. License description/remarks Microsoft Entra ID Plan 1 Required for delivering the standard services Microsoft Entra Suite Required for one or all optional services Microsoft Entra Private Access Required for the Microsoft Entra Private Access Optional Service Microsoft Entra Internet Access Required for the Microsoft Entra Internet Access Optional Service

Section.name: Location
Section.Content: This service is not delivered from within the Customer premises. Only from the Cegeka (virtual) Office Environment.

Section.name: Ownership
Section.Content: All automation used to deliver this service remains the property of Cegeka at all times.

Section.name: Miscellaneous
Section.Content: N/A

Section.name: RACI Matrix
Section.Content: Listed below are the activities related to the Service; the activities RACI related to enabling services are listed in the Enabling Services Document. ACTIVITY CUSTOMER CEGEKA STANDARD SERVICE - MANAGEMENT OF THE SECURE ACCESS PLATFORM Maintain the Global Secure Access management platform Provide pro-active advise about the use of Global Secure Access Maintain Microsoft 365 Traffic Profile within Global Secure Access Maintain on Global Secure Access clients Provide Dashboard of the Global Secure Access Platform Technical L2/L3 incident handling OPTIONAL SERVICE - INTERNET ACCESS Management of the Entra Internet Access Platform Maintain Internet Access Features Provide Dashboard of the Internet Access Technical L2/L3 incident handling for Entra Internet Access Platform OPTIONAL SERVICE - Private Access Maintain Private Access Features Maintain Private Access Traffic Profile within Global Secure Access Maintain Private Access Connectors Provide Dashboard of the Private Access RACI Role definition = Responsible: Party responsible for performing the activity and/or achieve the task. One party is Responsible, although other Parties can be delegated to assist in the actual work if required. An Accountable must sign off (approve) on work that Responsible provides. Responsible is by default proactive. = Accountable: Party ultimately accountable for the correct and thorough completion of the task and/or activity as performed by Responsible. For each task and/or activity maximum one Accountable must be specified. Accountable has the mandate to accept/dismiss on work provided by Responsible. Accountable is by default reactive. = Consulted: Party to be consulted (whose opinions are sought in two-way communication) when performing tasks and/or activities. Consulted may also provide requirements on scope, result expectations, etc. Consulted is by default reactive. = Informed: Party to be informed/kept up-to-date on tasks and/or activities (just one-way communication). Informed often only on completion of the task and/or activity. Informed no formal role in realization and therefore by default reactive.

Section.name: Onboarding
Section.Content: (Technical) Implementation Phase This document only describes the implementation steps required for this service. The governance activities around the implementation of this service are not part of this approach and need to be added manually based on the customer project. The implementation phase of the project consists of the following: In the following paragraphs these phases will be described in more detail. Initiation & Design This phase begins with a face-to-face session where the customer defines the requirements for the implementation. During this session, Cegeka will present the standard approach for implementing the service and its components. The standard approach includes a standard design with predefined design choices. Based on the requirements discussed in the session, adjustments to the design can be made where allowed by Cegeka's standards. Additionally, it will be determined which optional services will be implemented along with the base service. This phase has the following set of minimal activities: The results of the phase should be the following documents: Technical Design of the service and a list acceptation criteria for the implementation of the service approved by the customer. Implementation & Testing During this phase, the base service will be implemented along with any additional services. Cegeka will perform the implementation based on the designs prepared in the previous phase. The process begins with implementing and testing the base services. This includes enabling client installation, which will be configured within Microsoft Intune where feasible. Once the base services are implemented, validated, and tested, the additional services will be added and configured. When the additional service Internet Access is provided, the necessary policies will be established as part of the service setup. These policies will be enabled through the customer's Conditional Access policies. Any activities required to modify the conditional access policies will be included in the implementation of this service. The additional service, Private Access, will begin with the deployment of connectors to route traffic towards private applications within the customer's environment. Once the connectors are activated within Global Secure Access, an initial quick access configuration will be provided. This can later be translated into a per-application configuration using application discovery. Application discovery will identify applications, and their traffic based on the usage of the quick access configuration. This phase has the following set of minimal activities: Production Rollout The production rollout of this service will consist of the deployment of the Global Secure Access client to all operating systems in scope of the project. After the agents are deployed the Microsoft 365 Traffic profile can be implemented by activating the corresponding conditional access policy For Internet Access the additional conditional access policies will be activated and monitored. For Private Access the first step is activating the Quick Access configuration. Based on the data additional per application policies will be created. This can be done during the project phase or when this service is running. When this service is in the run phase, changes per application need to be created. In the project phase, this can be coordinated by the project manager and no change per application is required. This phase has the following set of minimal activities: Service Onboarding Requirements This service has the following onboarding requirements which need to be in place before this service:

Section.name: Service Window
Section.Content: Table below gives an overview of the applicable Service Support Windows for each Standard & Optional service: Service windows Description Service CEGEKA OFFICE HOURS 8x5: 9AM – 5PM CET, Monday up to and including Friday – business day Applicable for standard and optional service CEGEKA EXTENDED OFFICE HOURS 12x5: 7AM – 7PM CET, Monday up to and including Friday – business day [N/A] CEGEKA 24X7 24x7 [N/A]

Section.name: Service Level
Section.Content: Definition The availability of this service is the ability from workplaces to connect to the Global Access Service and be able to connect to resources (Microsoft 365, Internet or Private). The availability of these resources themselves is not part of this service. Example: When the users of a private access application can connect but the application does not accept the connection this service KPI will be held. Calculation Service Window Cegeka Office Hours Assumptions N/A Frequency Per Month Objectives (SLA) To measure of the performance of the service, the following KPIs will be used: STANDARD SERVICE - Management of the Global Secure Access Platform: OPTIONAL SERVICE - Internet Access OPTIONAL SERVICE - Private Access 3 rd Party SLA This service makes use of a third party PAAS provider. For the SLA’s concerning uptime and/or availability of the 3 rd party platform we refer to the following documentation documentation Service Level Agreements (SLA) for Online Services .

Section.name: Service Billing
Section.Content: Measurement The quantity of each billing element is determined on a mentioned monthly basis, on the 1 st day of the month: Calculation / Billing The price(s) is/are determined in the pricing schedule where this Service is residing. The Service is billed per billing element:

Section.name: Standard Service Billing
Section.Content: id Component Billing element BILLING UNIT frequency PRD.7.4.007.01 Management of the Secure Access Platform Standard Service Per User Per Month

Section.name: Optional Service Billing
Section.Content: id Component Billing element BILLING UNIT frequency PRD.7.4.007.02 Internet Access Optional Service Per User Per Month PRD.7.4.007.03 Private Access Optional Service Per User Per Month

Section.name: Service Request Billing
Section.Content: id SERVICE REQUEST TYPE SERVICE REQUEST BILLING method (*) approval method (**) STANDARD CHANGE None STANDARDIZED CHANGE None non STANDARD CHANGE Non Standard Change Install / Update Global Secure Access Client version T&M Approval/CAB Non Standard Change Remove / Rollback Global Secure Access Client version T&M Approval/CAB Non Standard Change Create Security Profile T&M Approval/CAB Non Standard Change Update Security Profile T&M Approval/CAB Non Standard Change Remove Security Profile T&M Approval/CAB Non Standard Change Create Web Content Filtering Rule T&M Approval/CAB Non Standard Change Update Web Content Filtering Rule T&M Approval/CAB Non Standard Change Remove Web Content Filtering Rule T&M Approval/CAB Non Standard Change Create Connector Group T&M Approval/CAB Non Standard Change Install and add new Connector T&M Approval/CAB Non Standard Change Update Connector(s) T&M Approval/CAB Non Standard Change Create Application T&M Approval/CAB Non Standard Change Grant Access to existing Application T&M Approval/CAB Non Standard Change Remove Access to existing Application T&M Approval/CAB Non Standard Change Remove Application T&M Approval/CAB Non Standard Change Remove and delete Connector T&M Approval/CAB Non Standard Change Remove Connector Group T&M Approval/CAB (*) Billing Method : (**) Approval Method:

Section.name: Relation to Enabling Services
Section.Content: To guarantee the Service quality Cegeka refers to the Enabling Services from the contract document set and their corresponding standard KPI's. The following table lists only relevant specifics regarding enabling services for the product. When in the table no specific is filled, this implies that the service adheres to the standards described in the Enabling Services document. Enabling SERVICE RELATION Support Make use of the Cegeka support ‘processes’ to be able to deliver and manage the product. Event Management Incident Management Problem Management Service Desk Request & Change Management Make use of the Cegeka processes Request and Change management to be able to deliver and manage the product. Request Management Change Management Patch Management Software Release Management Operations Make use of the following Cegeka ‘operational’ activities/processes. Operations/Housekeeping Capacity Management Availability Management Hardening Data Management Disaster Recovery Management Life Cycle Management ICT Continuity Management Configuration Management Knowledge & Documentation Management (Information) Security Management Privileged Identity and Access Management Manage Continuous Delivery Make use of the following Cegeka tool(s). Cegeka Customer Portal Reporting & Dashboarding Standards Make use of the following Cegeka Standards. Compliance & Risk Assurances (incl. ISAE/ISO reporting & Compliance
